description:
| Class Code (Level 4) | 4100 | 4211 | 4212 | 4220 |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Class Name | Managed grassland | Semi-natural grassland with woody plants (CCD ≥ 30 %) | Semi-natural grassland without woody plants (CCD < 30 %) | Alpine & sub-alpine natural grassland |
| Area (HA) (2006) | 4.994.954 | 784.537 | 4.603.982 | 886.929 |
| Change 2006–2012 | -43.464 (-0.87 %) | +1.962 (+0.25 %) | -20.970 (-0.46 %) | -142 (-0.02 %) |
| Area (HA) (2012) | 4.951.490 | 786.500 | 4.583.012 | 886.787 |
| Change 2012–2018 | -40.204 (-0.81 %) | +75 (+0.01 %) | -46.135 (-1.01 %) | -265 (-0.03 %) |
| Area (HA) (2018) | 4.911.286 | 786.575 | 4.536.877 | 886.522 |
This table presents the area in hectares (HA) and the percentage change for four Level 4 grassland classes from 2006 to 2018, broken down into two periods: 2006–2012 and 2012–2018. Managed grassland (Class 4100) consistently showed the largest area, decreasing from 4,994,954 HA in 2006 to 4,911,286 HA in 2018, with percentage losses of -0.87% and -0.81% in the respective periods. Semi-natural grassland without woody plants (Class 4212) also experienced significant area reduction, from 4,603,982 HA in 2006 to 4,536,877 HA in 2018, with a notable decrease of -1.01% between 2012 and 2018. In contrast, Semi-natural grassland with woody plants (Class 4211, with Canopy Cover Density (CCD) ≥ 30 %) saw a slight increase in area over both periods, from 784,537 HA in 2006 to 786,575 HA in 2018. Alpine & sub-alpine natural grassland (Class 4220) showed minor area losses in both periods.
